title: Responses, Problem Details, Streaming, and Protocol-Aware Output · Runtime Contract: Writeheader Implicit Commit
summary: A service writes bodies before status and headers, exposes stack traces, treats cancellation as 500, flushes unbounded streams, and assumes optional ResponseWriter interfaces always exist. Planned artifact: Add and verify the httpsgo writeheader implicit commit increment in single-commit response and streaming contract. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Predict implicit 200, content sniffing, automatic length, one final status, informational responses, and header commitment order.
Understand server-owned body closure, early rejection, draining and connection reuse, trailers, compression expansion, and read-before-write compatibility.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.
